verses.commentary: Peter identifies as apostle, not just witness. He writes to God's elect, scattered exiles (diaspora, διασπορά): believers dispersed by persecution, living as foreigners in a hostile world. Election precedes faith.

Election is Trinitarian: foreknown by the Father, sanctified by the Spirit, obedient to Jesus Christ. Sprinkling with blood echoes Exodus 24:8, the covenant ratification. Grace and peace multiply through knowledge of God.

Doxology erupts: praise be to God for new birth. Living hope (elpis zōsa, ἐλπὶς ζῶσα) is alive, not wishful thinking. Hope is grounded in the resurrection of Christ. Dead Messiah means dead hope; risen Christ means living hope.

The inheritance is imperishable (aphtharton, ἄφθαρτον), undefiled, unfading. Earth's treasures corrode, decay, lose value. Heaven's inheritance is secure, kept by God for His people. Security rests in God's power, not human effort.

Believers are shielded (phroureo, φρουρέω): guarded by military watch. Faith is the means, God's power is the cause. Salvation is ready to be revealed in the last time. Present protection, future consummation.

In this (living hope, secure inheritance, divine protection) you rejoice. Now introduces contrast: temporary grief in various trials. Joy and sorrow coexist. Trials do not negate hope; they refine it.

Trials prove faith genuine (dokimion, δοκίμιον): tested and approved. Faith is more valuable than gold, which perishes despite refinement. Refined faith results in praise, glory, and honor at Christ's revelation. Suffering has eschatological purpose.

They have not seen Christ but love Him. Belief without sight is the essence of faith (cf. John 20:29). Inexpressible and glorious joy (agalliasthe, ἀγαλλιᾶσθε) is the result. Love without seeing, joy without meeting. Faith transcends experience.

The goal (telos, τέλος) of faith is salvation of souls. Present faith leads to future consummation. Salvation is process (being saved) and destination (will be saved). Receiving implies both gift and arrival.

The prophets searched intently (exeraúnao, ἐξεραυνάω): investigated diligently. They spoke of grace coming to believers but did not fully understand. Prophecy pointed beyond itself to Christ. Prophets served a future generation.

The Spirit of Christ testified in the prophets about Messiah's sufferings and glories. Suffering precedes glory (Luke 24:26). Prophets searched for the time and circumstances. They saw partially; we see fulfillment.

Prophets served not themselves but future believers. The gospel has now been preached by the Spirit. Even angels long to look into (parakúpto, παρακύπτω) these things: bend over to examine closely. Redemption is the cosmic drama that angels study but do not experience.

Therefore: in light of this salvation, prepare your minds (anazōnnúmi, ἀναζώννυμι): gird up, tuck in garments for action. Be sober (nēpho, νήφω): alert, self-controlled. Set hope fully on grace coming at Christ's revelation. Hope is active, not passive.

As obedient children, do not conform (syschēmatízo, συσχηματίζω) to former desires. Ignorance characterized pre-conversion life. Knowledge of God transforms desires. Obedience flows from new identity as God's children.

Be holy as God is holy. Holiness (hagios, ἅγιος) means set apart, distinct. God's character defines the standard. Holiness is not compartmentalized but total: in all you do. Imitation of God is the believer's calling.
Leviticus 11:44-45 quoted. Holiness is rooted in God's nature, not human effort. The imperative rests on the indicative: be what God is. Holiness reflects relationship with the Holy One.

God judges impartially (aprosōpolēmptos, ἀπροσωπολήμπτως): without favoritism. He is Father but also Judge. Live as foreigners (paroikia, παροικία): temporary residents. Reverent fear (phobos, φόβος) is not terror but awe. Believers fear disappointing the Father who redeemed them.

Redemption (lytroō, λυτρόω) means release by payment of ransom. Not with perishable silver or gold. Redemption from empty way of life: futile, meaningless existence inherited from ancestors. Traditions and religion cannot save; only Christ's blood redeems.

Precious blood of Christ, a lamb without blemish or defect. Passover imagery: the lamb must be perfect (Exodus 12:5). Christ is the unblemished sacrifice. His sinlessness qualifies Him as substitute. Precious (timios, τίμιος) means invaluable, beyond price.

Christ was foreknown (proginōsko, προγινώσκω) before creation but revealed in these last times. The cross was not Plan B. Redemption was purposed in eternity, executed in history. For your sake: salvation is personal, intentional.

Through Christ, believers trust God. Christ is mediator. God raised and glorified Him, validating His work. Faith and hope rest in God because of Christ's resurrection. No resurrection, no faith (1 Corinthians 15:14).

Purified by obeying the truth results in sincere love (anypókritos, ἀνυπόκριτος): unhypocritical, genuine. Truth sanctifies (John 17:17). Love one another deeply (ektenos, ἐκτενῶς), from a pure heart. Vertical purification produces horizontal love.

Born again (anagennaō, ἀναγεννάω): regenerated, not of perishable seed but imperishable. Natural birth is temporary; spiritual birth is eternal. The living and enduring word of God is the means. The gospel generates new life.

Isaiah 40:6-8 quoted. All flesh is like grass, temporary and fragile. Glory fades like flowers. Human achievement and beauty wither. The contrast highlights the permanence of God's word. Mortality meets immortality.

The word of the Lord endures forever. This is the word preached to you: the gospel. Temporary people receive eternal truth. Perishable lives are born again through imperishable seed. The gospel outlasts all human endeavor.
